name: Speech Recognition (Speech Framework)
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Speech Recognition (Speech Framework)

APPROACH:
- Use SFSpeechRecognizer for on-device speech-to-text.
- Two modes: (a) live audio from microphone via AVAudioEngine, (b) pre-recorded audio file via SFSpeechURLRecognitionRequest.
- For live transcription, use SFSpeechAudioBufferRecognitionRequest with AVAudioEngine.

PERMISSIONS (CRITICAL — both are required for live mic):
1. NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription in Info.plist — required for Speech framework.
2. NSMicrophoneUsageDescription in Info.plist — required for AVAudioEngine microphone access.
3. Request authorization: SFSpeechRecognizer.requestAuthorization { status in ... }
4. Request mic permission: AVAudioSession.sharedInstance().requestRecordPermission { granted in ... }
5. Check both before starting — show clear UI if either is denied.

LIVE TRANSCRIPTION FLOW:
1. Create AVAudioEngine and get its inputNode.
2. Configure audio session: .setCategory(.record, mode: .measurement, options: .duckOthers), then .setActive(true).
3. Create SFSpeechAudioBufferRecognitionRequest, set shouldReportPartialResults = true.
4. Install a tap on inputNode: inputNode.installTap(onBus: 0, bufferSize: 1024, format: inputNode.outputFormat(forBus: 0)) { buffer, _ in request.append(buffer) }
5. audioEngine.prepare(); try audioEngine.start()
6. speechRecognizer.recognitionTask(with: request) { result, error in ... } — update UI with result.bestTranscription.formattedString.
7. On stop: audioEngine.stop(); inputNode.removeTap(onBus: 0); request.endAudio().

CRITICAL GOTCHAS:
- SFSpeechRecognizer availability can change (network-dependent for some languages). Implement SFSpeechRecognizerDelegate.speechRecognizer(_:availabilityDidChange:).
- For on-device only: set request.requiresOnDeviceRecognition = true (iOS 13+). This limits languages but works offline.
- Recognition tasks have a ~60-second limit for continuous recognition. For longer sessions, stop and restart the task periodically.
- ALWAYS remove the audio tap before installing a new one — calling installTap twice without removeTap crashes with 'com.apple.coreaudio.avfaudio' required condition is false: nullptr == Tap() (CreateRecordingTap). At the START of startRecording(), call inputNode.removeTap(onBus: 0) before installTap so a previous session never leaves a tap installed.
- Create a NEW SFSpeechAudioBufferRecognitionRequest for each recording session — do not reuse.
- When the app ALSO uses MusicKit or playback: fully stop speech (engine.stop(), removeTap(onBus: 0), endAudio()) BEFORE starting ApplicationMusicPlayer or any playback. Do not leave the mic tap installed while starting playback — that can cause applicationQueuePlayer timeout and CreateRecordingTap crashes.

UI BEST PRACTICES:
- Show a pulsing mic indicator while recording.
- Display partial results in real-time (grayed out or italic) and final results in normal text.
- Provide a clear Start/Stop toggle button.
- Show "Speech recognition unavailable" with a retry option when the recognizer reports unavailability.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT forget NSMicrophoneUsageDescription — the app will crash on mic access without it.
- Do NOT reuse SFSpeechAudioBufferRecognitionRequest across sessions.
- Do NOT call installTap without first calling removeTap(onBus: 0). At the start of startRecording() call inputNode.removeTap(onBus: 0) so a tap is never left installed from a previous run. Do NOT start MusicKit/playback while the speech tap is still installed — stop speech and removeTap first.
- Do NOT assume speech recognition works in Simulator — it requires a real microphone.
- Do NOT block the main thread with audio engine operations.

COMPILER: Any class using @Published must import Combine or you get 'unknown attribute Published'.
antiPatterns: Forgetting NSMicrophoneUsageDescription (app crashes on mic access)
Running audio engine operations on the main thread

commonErrors: CreateRecordingTap nullptr == Tap() crash (always call inputNode.removeTap(onBus: 0) before installTap; stop speech and removeTap before starting MusicKit playback)
